item.title: Musk threatened to make OpenAI’s Altman and Brockman ‘the most hated men in America’
item.description: Text exchange revealed in court filings came after world’s richest man tried to start last-minute settlement talks

item.title: The oil price crunch is looming
item.description: The longer the Strait of Hormuz blockade continues, the more likely a crisis-like adjustment in rich economies becomes

item.title: Trump family crypto venture sues major backer for defamation
item.description: World Liberty Financial accusation against Justin Sun comes after he sues firm over alleged fraud

item.title: Gamestop’s Ryan Cohen is either the new Buffett or the anti-Buffett
item.description: Pandemic meme stock’s boss today runs a pile of cash more than a company

item.title: Peter Thiel backs $1bn ocean data centre start-up powered by waves
item.description: US tech billionaire leads $140mn investment into Panthalassa as search for AI power pushes into exotic new frontiers

item.title: Alphaville’s tepid takes on GameStop vs eBay
item.description: We are highly confident that this could end up a car crash

item.title: Trial of Blackpool’s block trade king begins in Hong Kong
item.description: Simon Sadler of Segantii Capital faces criminal charges over alleged insider trading of clothing retailer Esprit

item.title: Scotch truce should give US trade partners a dram of hope
item.description: The industry offers a glaring example of the self-defeating nature of tariffs

item.title: EU blocks funds for key Chinese solar energy parts
item.description: Brussels cites security concerns in latest crackdown on sensitive China-made imports

item.title: Blackstone and Goldman among backers for $1.5bn Anthropic JV
item.description: New consulting company to advise Wall Street groups on how to deploy its AI across their investment portfolios

item.title: FTAV’s further reading
item.description: Drew Dickson on tech hype; Goldman on private credit; Smithsonian on Twister; ‘This is Fine’ dog on Steam

item.title: Is the US truly resilient to the energy market strife?
item.description: ‘Somewhat counterintuitive’ indeed

item.title: Canada’s beef producers say Mercosur trade deal will devastate industry
item.description: Farmers fear a flood of cheap meat as Carney government looks for entry to South American bloc

item.title: America’s small businesses left out of Trump tariff refunds
item.description: New procedures only reimburse importers, not those who indirectly sourced products through them

item.title: GameStop makes unsolicited $56bn offer for eBay
item.description: Video game retailer offers $125 a share for resale web giant

item.title: Our pension funds are not quasi-government money
item.description: New legislation pushing the mandatory allocation of investments into private assets conflicts with the fiduciary duty of trustees

item.title: AI + Halo = $$$
item.description: A techno-industrial boomlet

item.title: More than double the gas stuck in Hormuz is wasted each year, IEA says
item.description: Capping methane emissions would alleviate energy and climate crisis

item.title: FirstFT: US to ‘guide’ ships out of Hormuz, says Trump
item.description: Also in today’s newsletter: Airlines slash 2mn seats and EU urged to adapt to economic shifts

item.title: AI in Practice
item.description: Water utilities jettison listening sticks; restaurants aim to cut waste and reduce costs; recruiters’ quest to find the perfect connection; start-ups move fast with AI-generated code; hedge funds seek an edge; wealth managers insist AI can work in their favour

item.title: UAE fertiliser giant resorts to trucks to shift product out of Gulf
item.description: Abu Dhabi’s Fertiglobe switches to land cargo to avoid Strait of Hormuz as soaring prices allow it to shoulder extra costs

item.title: Greg Abel tells Berkshire holders, be patient
item.description: Plus, Elliott likens Iran war to battling Nazis, Wall Street piles into Treasuries, and Raphael at The Met

item.title: Airlines slash flights as fuel shortage fears mount
item.description: Millions of seats cut as Middle East crisis throws global travel into disarray

item.title: UK pub groups betting on World Cup to counter sluggish consumer demand
item.description: Optimism comes as a result of early bookings despite the sector enduring tepid sales growth in March

item.title: How the UK’s ‘King of Trainers’ ran into trouble
item.description: The athleisure boom turned JD Sports into a global powerhouse but slowing sales have led to conflict in the boardroom

item.title: The law firm that shaped Wall Street and the world
item.description: With fewer than 300 lawyers, Wachtell racked up bumper profits and exercised outsize influence. Now its model is being challenged as never before

item.title: Heat pump sales jump as consumers recoil at high fossil fuel prices
item.description: High demand for units in Europe suggests green technologies benefiting from energy price surge due to Iran war

item.title: ‘It’s crucial’: how AI is reshaping the fragrance industry
item.description: From hyper-personalisation to cutting costs, artificial intelligence is getting a hold on your perfume cabinet

item.title: Lessons from the agentic AI trailblazers
item.description: Many businesses have yet to deploy agents, so what tips can early adopters offer?

item.title: The land of milk and no money: UK farmers are in a fix
item.description: Prices are falling due to a glut of the white stuff, yet the costs of feed, fertilisers and fuel are rising
